channel.title: Lysandros personal website
channel.description: Recent content on Lysandros personal website

item.title: About
item.description:
👋 Hi, I’m Lysandros, a data engineer passionate about building clean, scalable data pipelines and backend systems that power real-world applications.

I began my career as a data analyst, where I discovered a deep interest in coding and problem-solving. That experience led me into data engineering, where I now work on transforming raw data into structured, actionable insights.

I’m currently deepening my knowledge of computer science, backend engineering, and modern data systems through structured programs like the Backend Path on Boot.dev. I’m especially interested in the intersection of software engineering and data infrastructure.

item.title: Certifications
item.description:
🧠 Computer Science & Fundamentals Link to heading

  • CS50’s Introduction to Computer Science – Harvard University
    Issued: Dec 2024
    View Certificate

  • PH125.2x: Data Science – Visualization – HarvardX via edX
    Issued: May 2019
    View Certificate

  • PH125.3x: Data Science – Probability – HarvardX via edX
    Issued: May 2019
    View Certificate

  • PH125.1x: Data Science – R Basics – HarvardX via edX
    Issued: Apr 2019
    View Certificate

  • PH526x: Using Python for Research – HarvardX via edX
    Issued: Dec 2018
    View Certificate

item.title: Projects
item.description:
🌟 Featured Projects Link to heading

Vehicle Routing Problem Solver (Harvard CS50) Link to heading

A web application that solves complex Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) scenarios for logistics optimization. Uses Google OR-Tools for route planning and NetworkX for graph operations. This was also the final project of Harvard’s CS50.

Tech Used: Python (Flask), Google OR-Tools, NetworkX, Geopy
🔗 GitHub · 🎥 Demo

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Skills Link to heading

Python; SQL; R; ETL/ELT; Data Modeling; Data Warehousing (Snowflake); Apache Spark (PySpark, Databricks); Data Orchestration (Airflow, Dataiku, Prefect); CI/CD & DevOps (Git, Docker); BI & Analytics Tools (Power BI); Agile/Kanban methodology; Streamlit; LLM API Integration (Anthropic Claude)

Experience Link to heading

Autoflows, Copenhagen, Denmark — Data Engineer
OCT 2025 - PRESENT

  • Built the Regex Validation Tool — a Streamlit app that shifted regex-based service marking management from an engineering bottleneck to a self-service workflow for the Support/CS team, covering 300+ dealerships across 8 European markets. Integrated Claude AI for pattern suggestions, Airflow DAG triggering for live deployment, and unit tests (pytest) to ensure pattern validation reliability.
  • Developed and maintained sampling pipelines for enterprise automotive clients. Authored full Confluence documentation for each pipeline; served as primary engineering contact for support escalations across these accounts.
  • Extended the team’s Python shared library with full CRUD support for NocoDB and Google Sheets additional methods, enabling cleaner configuration management across pipelines.
  • Resolved a high volume of production bugs spanning DMS adapter failures and CX pipeline issues across Norway, Sweden, Denmark, Estonia, Germany, Spain, Italy, and UK/Ireland.
  • Authored the team’s Onboarding Setup Guide and Coding Style Recommendations on Confluence, reducing new-hire ramp-up friction and improving cross-team code consistency.

Tech used: Python, Airflow, R, Streamlit, Git, NocoDB
